projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The persistent 18-19 mobile device count overnight with zero camera or audio activity, now continuing at wake-up time, strongly indicates a network scanning artifact or a large group of overnight guests who haven't yet triggered motion sensors; verify the network device list or confirm guest status before automating morning routines.

The stable 18-19 device count overnight, combined with zero camera or audio activity, indicates a persistent background state (likely overnight guests or IoT devices misclassified as mobile) rather than an active event or security concern.

The presence count is abnormally high (18-20 devices) for a four-person household at 4 AM, and the fluctuating count combined with zero camera activity suggests a potential network anomaly or unauthorized device connection that warrants investigation.

The presence count consistently fluctuates between 16-21 devices with zero camera activity at 11 PM Sunday, indicating the high count is likely due to IoT/smart home device network scanning rather than human presence or guests. This prevents false alerts about unexplained activity.

The mobile device count consistently fluctuates between 16 and 19 throughout the morning, significantly exceeding the household's typical device count, which strongly suggests a gathering of guests or extended family is present. This provides useful context for the day's activity, though no security concerns are indicated.

The presence count consistently toggles between 21 and 22 devices throughout the night with zero camera or activity signals, indicating a likely network artifact or a single device cycling its connection rather than actual household movement. This warrants a quick network check to prevent false alerts or unnecessary battery drain.

The persistence of 21-22 connected devices with zero camera motion from Friday evening through Saturday past midnight confirms a large group of guests is settled inside the home, likely resting in camera-blind zones, indicating a multi-night stay rather than a transient gathering.

The sustained 18-22 device count paired with zero camera motion indicates a large group of stationary occupants or an IoT counting anomaly, as it far exceeds the household's typical device footprint. This suggests a possible gathering or sensor misreporting that warrants verification.

The sustained 18-21 device count with zero camera motion from 19:12 to 20:43 on a Friday evening strongly indicates a large group of guests or extended family gathered in a camera-blind area, confirming a planned social event rather than a security concern.

The sustained 17-21 device count with zero camera motion over 90+ minutes strongly indicates dormant IoT devices or network polling artifacts rather than active human presence, but warrants monitoring for unrecorded guests or a network misconfiguration.
